## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about hybrid evergreen webinars. This is a stage that usually happens before you get into full blown evergreen webinar mode. It's a great way to help in that transition. As well as, by the way, this is also a technique that can be used within a regular evergreen webinar sequence. Also, I'll show you that in a moment. So one of the biggest problems with evergreen webinars is that you lose the engagement of answering questions live. And more importantly, you build the relationship of trust that's built through that process of them asking questions, you answering, fulfilling their particular needs at that particular moment. And that's one of the biggest problems with evergreen webinars is that people struggle to generate that trust with cold traffic to the level that it needs to be to be able to sell to them effectively. So a possible solution is this hybrid evergreen webinar system. So here's how they work. The webinar runs at a certain time. I don't know, I haven't figured out a way to be able to do what I'm going to show you here with webinars running at any time someone wants them to run. So the webinar starts at a certain time. Let's say 4pm on Wednesday. They are watching a recording. You're not there presenting live. You're doing whatever you want to during that period of time and you have an alarm set and you drop everything and you go so that you're there and ready to go exactly at the end of that webinar and do you answer their questions? As if you had been on their live the entire time. Now when you're working with a hybrid evergreen webinar, there's actually two options to test. The first one is what I call the invisible hybrid. Basically they have no idea what's actually happening. I'll show you this in a moment. Or the other one is an explicit hybrid where you tell them exactly what's going on. So an invisible hybrid, you need everything to be precisely seamless because you don't tell them that they're actually watching a recording. They think they're watching you live. And you do everything so that it just looks and feels like it's exactly the same. So if things like if you use your camera during the recorded webinar, your appearance needs to be exactly the same when you come on live. It needs to have the same clothes, same haircut, lighting in the same setting. Everything needs to be exactly the same, which also says if you're going to do a hybrid webinar and it's going to be an invisible hybrid, you may not want to show your face during the first part of the webinar,

## [03:00 - 06:00]

which by the way doesn't help build trust. So this is not my favorite by the way. And you need to work very precisely so you have absolutely no lag time between the end of the webinar and you being there to start answering questions. The other option is the explicit hybrid. Now the first time I saw this, I saw Frank Kern do it and I went, oh, that's brilliant. So here's what he did. He came on and he said, hi, this is Frank. So here's what's happening. I got this little girl, my cute little daughter. It's her bedtime. So what I've done is I'm going to put my daughter to bed because that's the priority in my life. Meanwhile, I'm going to show you a prerecorded first half of the webinar. But then after I put my daughter to bed, I'm going to show up on this webinar and answer all your questions. So yeah, for the next 48 minutes, you're going to be watching a recording. One of my team members will be answering the questions you type into the chat box live during that webinar. Then I'll show up after I put my daughter to bed and answer your questions in person. And you can be really explicit like he was or you can have some fun with it. You can say, hey, I'm a fishin' or whatever. You can do all kinds of things with it. It all depends on your own tone and manner. Those of you who know Frank know that he can pull things off that a lot of the rest of the world can't. But what it basically does is this takes off a lot of the pressure to have it look seamless. Now, yes, you are going to lose some people who say, I'm not listening to a recording. And so they'll just go away. But other people, especially if you handle it right at that first part, and you just like, I'm going to be here. I'm going to spend all the time you need to answer the questions in person in 48 minutes or whatever that time period is. So this option is great for the early days of setting up your ever-growing webinar, allowing you to figure out what's working and what's not by the questions that they're answering and how you're converting. It's a great way to be able to basically save cells by coming in answering questions. And oftentimes you can create cells that weren't going to be created from the recorded webinar. Frankly, I also like to use this as part of a pure evergreen funnel where people are getting recorded webinars. And sometimes I'll just say, hey, I'm running a live webinar on date and time to answer your questions. And it actually is a way to get people who don't buy your product on the recorded webinar, be able to come back and do another exposure to your webinar because you're going to be giving it live this particular time. So that's a hybrid evergreen webinar. It's an interesting idea.

## [06:00 - 06:09]

Not everyone will use it. Not everyone should use it, but it's an interesting concept and I have seen it work very well.
